channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Car park of Avonhurst House, 30th March
item.description:
Every week this happens. Why can you not install a camera?

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Coverdale Road, London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load (Estates)

item.title: Multiple large potholes covering the entire road of one side of traffic, 30th March
item.description:
The potholes in this location are extremely dangerous. It has covered the entire driving space of moving traffic. They are so bad that drivers feel worried which causes drivers to move into oncoming traffic lane to go around the damages. This is becoming really dangerous as the actions of many drivers will likely cause an accident with other vehicles by resorting to going around the potholes. If drivers go through the pothole, they are severely damaged roads which have a high chance of causing damages to own vehicles too.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 36, Goswell Road, City of London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Rubbish all over the street, 30th March
item.description:
Rubbish on street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Southwold Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: All of the street bins have disappeared, 30th March
item.description:
Street bins have been removed / taken.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 140, Maple Road, Penge, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van pulled up and dumped loads of materials, 30th March
item.description:
Witnessed a white van pull up and dump loads of rubbish and materials into the bins for both Princess Court and Crystal Court and leave. Have left bins in a state but not able to take a photo currently, have a video and registration plate.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): College Road, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Phone box to first house on LittleHeath Rd, 30th March
item.description:
Small shards of glass over pavement and against fencing larger pieces of glass near phone box
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Little Heath Road, Upper Belvedere, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Drumped Green Waste bag - ref 9167238, 30th March
item.description:
If the contractor had in fact come out to the original report, it cannot be missed. It’s is still there on the crossroads of Marion/Austin and Sidmouth BR52DF. It’s been joined by a whole host of dumped litter since. With a 5% increase in tax this shouldn’t be so hard to find. Thank you.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 37, Marion Crescent, Poverest, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Lamp post out of hole, 30th March
item.description:
Some young kids came along and pulled the lamp post out of its hole. Could hit someone or a car.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Crescent, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Bedding left at empty house garden front, 30th March
item.description:
Left here by a rough sleeper weeks ago

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Gore Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Damaged traffic island, 30th March
item.description:
Tweedy road junction of widmore / Kentish way. This island was damaged months ago. Why has it not been fixed. Very dangerous
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Tweedy Road, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
item.category: Pavement Defect (uneven surface / cracked paving slab)

Pieces of wood left outside 111 Kirkham Street

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Kirkham Street, Plumstead

item.title: Rubbish, 30th March
item.description:
Someone threw their rubbish all there when it is a place where people walk and past by.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Old Kent Road, Old Kent Road, Greater London

item.title: Street lamp not working, 30th March
item.description:
Street lamp out at the bottom of the hill/overpass, making it difficult and dangerous to see other vehicles.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Arbuthnot Lane, Blendon, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Light is directly outside bedroom window (No.52 Leckwith), 30th March
item.description:
Light illuminates bedroom at night, despite wooden blinds.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Leckwith Avenue, West Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Abandoned moped has been damaged., 30th March
item.description:
Today I noticed an abandoned moped that has been damaged with several pieces detached from the main body. The number plate would appear to be absent.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): East Rochester Way, Avery Hill, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Roadside kerb is not fixed securely outside number 13 ravenswood bexley DA53NN, 30th March
item.description:
This has been here for over 6 months now
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 12, Ravenswood, Albany Park, Greater London

item.title: Flood light out, 30th March
item.description:
Light above Starbucks
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 25, Market Square, Plaistow, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Overflowing bins following waste collection, 30th March
item.description:
Hello there, we are increasingly having problems of over flowing bins which means dog poo bags spill onto street and/or are more easily accessible for animals to riffle through. This happens here by the entrance to the community park,Grosvenor park side and on the corner of Urlwin street and grosvenor park. We are on a route for dog walkers going to burgess park. So lots of dog poo already. It’s unfortunate that the actions of those who do bag and bin it are undermined by over full bins. Many thanks

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Grosvenor Park, Camberwell, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Full event style red carpet dumped on Canon Beck Road, opposite Car Park, 30th March
item.description:
Full red carpet dumped on Canon Beck Road, out the back of the old Lord Nelson pub, opposite the car park

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 52, Canon Beck Road, Rotherhithe, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flyposting repeatedly appearing on boarded up pub, 30th March
item.description:
Flyposters attached to The Bolton pub, 326 Earl's Court Rd SW5 9BQ. Has been reported to council who got them removed but they keep being put up again. Pub is boarded up to prevent squatters. Due to be turned into boutique hotel in due course.
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 326, Old Brompton Road, Earl's Court, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
